questions.text: What is the primary function of heart valves?

answers.text: To increase the force of cardiac contraction
To ensure one-way flow of blood and prevent backflow
To generate electrical impulses for conduction
To supply oxygen to the myocardium

questions.text: A patient has a murmur caused by backflow of blood from the left ventricle into the left atrium. Which valve is most likely affected?
What prevents the AV valve cusps from prolapsing backward into the atria during ventricular contraction?

answers.text: Contraction of the papillary muscles pulling the chordae tendineae
The elastic recoil of the aorta

questions.text: During which phase of the cardiac cycle do the semilunar valves open?

answers.text: Ventricular systole when ventricular pressure exceeds arterial pressure

questions.text: Which circulation is described as a low-pressure system?
The left anterior descending artery (LAD) supplies blood to which region of the heart?
All coronary veins ultimately drain into which structure?

answers.text: The right atrium via the coronary sinus
The left atrium via the pulmonary veins

questions.text: Which artery supplies the right ventricle along the right margin of the heart?

answers.text: Marginal branch of the right coronary artery

questions.text: A paramedic needs to feel a pulse at the groin. Which artery are they palpating?
Which veins drain deoxygenated blood from the brain, face, and neck?
